name: Property-Based Testing

x.defectClass: Edge-case and invariant-violation bugs that example-based tests never enumerate (boundaries, unusual sizes, unicode, ordering).
x.oracle: A general property the code must satisfy for all inputs (round-trip, idempotence, agreement with a reference model) — not a specific expected output.
x.automationLevel: High — the framework generates inputs, runs the property, and shrinks failures to a minimal counterexample; the human writes generators and properties.
blindSpots: Only as strong as the properties written — weak ones pass vacuously
Non-determinism without recorded seeds harms reproducibility
Poor generators miss the interesting input space
x.cost: Higher authoring effort than examples and slower to run; shrinking and seed management add some maintenance.
x.lifecycleStage: Unit and integration stages during development and in CI; especially valuable for parsers, serialisers, data structures and protocol code.

def: Tests assert invariants over many automatically-generated inputs rather than fixed examples.
problem: Find edge cases example-based tests miss by checking general properties across random inputs.
mechanism: A generator produces randomised inputs; the framework checks a property holds, and on failure shrinks the input to a minimal counterexample.
myths: "Replaces example tests" — it complements them, not replaces
